seo.title: DSPy Guardrails for Education
seo.description: Compliance guide for DSPy agents in education. Meet FERPA, COPPA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Education teams using DSPy to build AI tutors and personalized learning systems face unique risks due to the high sensitivity of student data and strict privacy regulations. DSPy's optimization can lead to overfitting prompts that generate biased or inappropriate content, while chain-of-thought reasoning may produce plausible but incorrect educational logic. Vex provides essential guardrails by verifying DSPy module outputs against FERPA, COPPA, and accessibility standards at inference time.

complianceRequirements.description: Protects student education records from unauthorized disclosure, requiring parental consent for minors under 13 and strict data minimization.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies DSPy outputs to block PII extraction or sharing of student data without consent, flagging violations before exposure.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates verifiable parental consent for collecting personal information from children under 13 via online services.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans DSPy-generated responses for child-directed content containing PII, enforcing age-appropriate data handling.

complianceRequirements.description: State-specific laws like California's Student Online Personal Information Protection Act (SOPIPA) restrict AI use of student data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex custom policies detect and block DSPy outputs that violate state-specific data retention or sharing rules.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires educational content to be accessible to users with disabilities, including screen reader compatibility.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates DSPy-generated educational content for WCAG compliance and accessibility violations.

industryRisks.name: Student PII Exposure
industryRisks.description: DSPy-optimized tutors accidentally include student names, IDs, or performance data in public responses.
industryRisks.example: Chain-of-thought module references 'Johnny's math score of 78%' in a shared lesson plan.

industryRisks.name: Inappropriate Content Generation
industryRisks.description: Overfitted prompts produce age-inappropriate or biased educational material for minors.
industryRisks.example: AI tutor generates culturally insensitive history explanations due to training data bias.

industryRisks.name: Incorrect Instructional Logic
industryRisks.description: Chain-of-thought produces plausible but factually wrong teaching sequences.
industryRisks.example: Math tutor teaches '2+2=5' through flawed reasoning that evades basic assertions.

industryRisks.name: Accessibility Violations
industryRisks.description: Generated content fails screen reader compatibility or lacks alt text for visuals.
industryRisks.example: DSPy module outputs math equations in inaccessible image format only.

implementation.title: Wrap DSPy Tutor Module with FERPA Checks
implementation.description: Protect student data by verifying no PII appears in tutor responses using vex.verify() on forward() calls.

implementation.title: Age-Appropriate Content Validation
implementation.description: Ensure DSPy-generated lessons are suitable for minors with COPPA compliance checks.

implementation.title: Accessibility Verification for Educational Content
implementation.description: Validate DSPy outputs meet Section 508 standards before student exposure.

caseStudyScenario.heading: DSPy in Education: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
Maplewood School District deployed DSPy-powered AI tutors across 15 elementary schools serving 8,000 K-8 students. The system used DSPy ChainOfThought modules optimized on historical student interactions to generate personalized math and reading lessons. Teachers integrated the tutors into daily instruction, with student queries processed through composable DSPy modules that adapted explanations based on real-time performance data.

However, during optimization, prompts overfit to training examples containing student names and scores, causing the tutors to reference specific students ('Like Sarah's mistake yesterday') in lessons shared across classes. Chain-of-thought reasoning also produced plausible but incorrect fraction explanations, while math equation outputs lacked accessible formatting, violating Section 508.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex guardrails wrapped all DSPy forward() calls with FERPA, COPPA, and accessibility policies. The no_pii policy blocked 97% of PII exposures before reaching students, while coppa_safe validation ensured age-appropriate content. Accessibility checks forced math outputs into LaTeX format compatible with screen readers. Post-deployment audit showed zero compliance violations across 250,000 inferences, maintaining accreditation and avoiding $2.4M in potential FERPA fines.
